rationale: The page provides detailed information on the Axiom-4 mission, including India's participation with astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla. It also outlines specific ISRO research experiments conducted on the International Space Station (ISS) and provides general facts about the ISS and the Moon's far side.
cleanedText: Tidal Deformation: Earth’s gravity causes the nearside to flex more than the farside during the Moon’s orbit. This flexing shows structural differences between the two sides of the Moon. About the far side of the Moon: Refers to the hemisphere of the Moon that always faces away from Earth (hence also referred to as the dark side of the Moon). This happens because the Moon is tidally locked with Earth due to gravitational pull. It has a thicker crust, more craters, and fewer lava plains than near side. Reaching the Moon's far side is challenging due to its remoteness and rugged landscape, featuring massive craters and limited flat areas. 4.6. AXIOM-4 MISSION. Why in the News? The Axiom-4 Mission carrying Indian astronaut Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla and 3 other astronauts successfully returned on July 15, 2025. About Axiom-4 (Ax-4) Mission: Ax-4 is a 4th private astronaut mission, to the International Space Station (ISS), of private US Company Axiom Space in collaboration with NASA and SpaceX. It was launched aboard a SpaceX Dragon Spacecraft by the Falcon 9 launch vehicle. Aim: To “realize the return” to human spaceflight for India, Poland, and Hungary. Ax-4 marks each nation’s first mission to the ISS (except USA) in history and with each nation’s first government-sponsored flight in more than 40 years. Shubhanshu Shukla is the first Indian astronaut to board the ISS and only the second Indian to travel to space after Rakesh Sharma in 1984. Key Research and activities executed by ISRO: Crop growth: Study impact of microgravity on 6 crop seed varieties for future space farming. Cyanobacteria: Observe growth and activity for use in spacecraft life support systems. Cyanobacteria are aquatic bacteria that can photosynthesize. Space Microalgae: Compare metabolic and genetic activity in space vs Earth; potential use as food, fuel, or for life support. Myogenesis: Study muscle loss, identify pathways responsible for skeletal muscle dysfunction in microgravity and explore therapeutic targeting strategies. Tardigrades: Investigate the revival, survival, and reproduction of tardigrades (commonly known as water bears) to identify molecular mechanisms of resilience. About International Space Station (ISS): A habitable artificial satellite in Low Earth orbit (LEO). Key partners for ISS: Europe (ESA), the USA (NASA), Japan (JAXA), Canada (CSA) and Russia (Roscosmos). Largest artificial body in orbit providing a laboratory in space, having an orbit such that it can fly over 90% of inhabited Earth.
